channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Texas Braces for More Rain, Floods as Gulf Storm Strengthens
item.description: Tropical storm and flood warnings are in effect along the Texas and Louisiana coastlines as a weather system strengthens and threatens to bring torrential downpours.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Solar Surpasses Gas in California After Panel Installs Surge
item.description: Solar power is surpassing natural gas generation in California, demonstrating the sustained and growing impact of renewable energy in the biggest US market for photovoltaic panels.
item.dc:creator: Emily Forgash

item.title: US Home Solar Takes Hit After Losing Key Tax Credit
item.description: California and Florida are bucking the trend.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee, Zahra Hirji

item.title: European Heat Wave Pushes French Power Prices to 2023 High
item.description: A record-breaking heat wave is set to build in western Europe this week, leading to so-called tropical nights and driving French power prices higher as cooling demand surges.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Naureen S Malik

item.title: Scientists Found the Coral Reefs That Can Survive Climate Change
item.description: Some coral reef ecosystems have a far greater chance of surviving the effects of climate change than previously understood, according to new global research.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee

item.title: Floods Threaten Texas and Louisiana This Week
item.description: Louisiana and Texas, including Houston, face several days of heavy rain, bringing a risk of floods as a potential tropical storm tries to organize in the western Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Why Your Summer Tomatoes Cost So Much
item.description: Prices are soaring after crop losses in Mexico and Florida, offering a glimpse of how climate disruptions could make food inflation longer lasting.
item.dc:creator: Emma Court

item.title: US Residential Solar Installations Set to Stall for Years
item.description: While most of the country is experiencing a decline, two states are bucking the trend: California and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Zahra Hirji

item.title: EU Nations Approve Carbon Benchmarks for Handout of Free Permits
item.description: European Union member states approved a controversial update of benchmark values for the bloc’s carbon market, taking a key step toward distribution of free allowances to companies in the flagship emissions trading system.
item.dc:creator: Ewa Krukowska

item.title: US Finalizes $1.6 Billion Loan to Michigan Gas Utility DTE
item.description: The Trump administration has finalized a $1.6 billion loan to Michigan-based utility DTE Energy Co. to modernize hundreds of miles of natural gas infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ari Natter
